name: Nick Travis
abstract: Nick Travis (November 16, 1925, in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania – October 7, 1964, in New York City) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: 20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: I was a member of the original Sauter-Finegan Orchestra... Nick Travis played trumpet
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: Later on Conte left and Nick Travis came on, and Don Fagerquist. We had great trumpet players
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: I was drafted out of Woody’s band by the way. Me and Nick Travis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing with Mulligan... Nick Travis was calling the guys... Nick was first trumpet player with the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I knew I was going to have Nick... both he and Art played in the mutes
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: we used to call each other Deedle-ee and Nicholai and stuff like that
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
